items.title: FlowWAM: Optical Flow as a Unified Action Representation for World Action Models

items.title: Inside TPU and GPU Clusters: The Anatomy of Collective Communication

items.title: Boogu-Image-0.1: Boosting Open-Source Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation

items.title: TRACE: Turn-level Reward Assignment via Credit Estimation for Long-Horizon Agents

items.title: Ring-Zero: Scaling Zero RL to a Trillion Parameters for Emergent Reasoning

items.title: VideoRAE: Taming Video Foundation Models for Generative Modeling via Representation Autoencoders
items.summary: Video generative models commonly rely on latent spaces learned by 3D Variational Autoencoders (3D-VAEs). However, conventional 3D-VAEs are mainly optimized for pixel-level reconstruction, which can limit the semantic and spatio-temporal structure captured by their latents. Meanwh

items.title: Hy-Embodied-VLM-1.0: Efficient Physical-World Agents
items.summary: Building capable embodied agents requires not only multimodal perception and understanding, but also agentic capabilities for reasoning about actions, adapting to evolving situations, and interacting with the physical world. In this report, we introduce Hy-Embodied-VLM-1.0, an ef

items.title: Chinese Models vs. Frontier Models
items.summary: The Epoch Capabilities Index combines scores from many different AI benchmarks into a single "general capability" scale, and the chart below shows that open-weight models trail the closed-weight frontier by around four months.

items.title: Cover Ratios for Hyperscaler Bonds Declining
items.summary: The cover ratio measures how many dollars of investor orders a bond deal receives for every dollar of bonds issued.

items.title: How Exposed Is Your Job to AI? The Experts Can’t Agree
items.summary: To gauge how much AI affects a job, researchers rate it from 0 to 1 based on how many of its tasks could be, or already are, done with AI.

items.summary: Net foreign inflows into US equities have surged to a record high, driven in large part by overseas investors seeking AI exposure they cannot get in their home markets, see chart below.

items.summary: A new study from Ramp, linking observed AI spending to workforce records across 21,559 US firms, finds that companies adopting AI grow their headcount 10% over the two years following adoption.

items.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
items.summary: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

items.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
items.summary: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

items.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
items.summary: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

items.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
items.summary: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

items.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
items.summary: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

items.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
items.summary: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a $14.8 billion all-stock deal that would nearly double the company’s global footprint and create one of the world’s largest food-delivery platforms outside China.

items.title: Newsletter platform Beehiiv now lets subscribers chat with each other, adds AI
items.summary: Beehiiv is launching an AI Copilot to help publishers with user growth and analytics.

items.title: X cracks down on creators who steal content
items.summary: X will use Grok AI to better detect stolen content, redirect payouts to original creators, and crack down on engagement bait.
